title: Porous Navier-Stokes Configuration
description: Configuration schema for the Gridap-based Porous Navier-Stokes solver.

physical_properties.title: Physical Properties
physical_properties.description: Physical properties of the fluid and the porous medium, including numerical floor values and reaction terms.

f_x.description: Body force component in the x-direction (e.g., gravity).

f_y.description: Body force component in the y-direction.

physical_epsilon.description: Physical compressibility epsilon (ε_phys), added to the mass-equation LHS only (ε_phys·p). May be 0 (no physical compressibility). Must be >= 0.

numerical_epsilon.description: Numerical penalty epsilon (ε_num), the Codina iterative penalty. Lagging ε_num*p to the nonlinear iterate makes it cancel in the RESIDUAL and survive only as ε_num*dp in the JACOBIAN's pressure block — a Newton-step regularization that vanishes at convergence, so the physical/manufactured solution is unaltered (no consistency error, no outer loop). Provides pressure stability where the discretization lacks it (e.g. P2 in 3D). Bounded by ε_num <= C2*c1*inf{alpha^2*tau1/h^2} (article.tex eq:UpperBoundOnEpsilon) — larger destroys stability. 0 disables it. Must be >= 0.

sigma_constant.description: Constant term parameter for the reaction model.

sigma_linear.description: Linear drag coefficient (A) derived from Darcy's law.

sigma_nonlinear.description: Nonlinear drag coefficient (B) from Forchheimer correction.

u_base_floor_ref.description: Reference base velocity for regularization floor.

epsilon_floor.description: Absolute velocity magnitude cutoff to prevent divide-by-zero.

velocity_magnitude_derivative_floor.description: Additive floor on |u| regularizing the d|u|/du = u/|u| factor in the Exact-Newton derivative terms (dsigma/du, dtau/du) at a stagnation point. Jacobian-only (zeroed in Picard, absent from the residual), so it cannot move the converged solution. See theory/velocity_floor_regularization/.

tau_regularization_limit.description: Minimum limit applied to stabilization parameters (tau).

domain.title: Domain Configuration
domain.description: Geometrical description and dimensions of the domain and the spatial distribution of the porous medium.

r_1.description: Inner radius geometry control for porosity field.

r_2.description: Outer radius geometry control for porosity field.

bounding_box.description: Bounding box lengths for the geometry [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax].

numerical_method.title: Numerical Method
numerical_method.description: Discretization, stabilization, mesh parsing, and nonlinear solver components.

element_spaces.title: Element Spaces
element_spaces.description: Finite element type and order degrees.

equal_order_only.description: Flag used primarily in testing to enforce equal-order elements.

stabilization.title: Stabilization Configuration
stabilization.description: Mechanisms for stabilizing the Galerkin solver.

element_size.description: Element characteristic size h(K) convention feeding the stabilization parameters tau_1/tau_2 (src/geometry.jl). 'volume' = legacy grid-spacing proxy (d!*|K|)^(1/d): sqrt(2*Area) on triangles, sqrt(Area) on quads, (6*V)^(1/3) on tets. 'shortest_edge' = the minimum EDGE length (true edges only, excludes quad/hex diagonals) = Codina's convention, and equals the grid spacing on the structured simplex mesh (DEFAULT). 'average_edge' = mean edge length. 'diameter' = strict mathematical element diameter max||x_i - x_j|| over ALL vertex pairs (the paper reports h = sqrt(2)/N, i.e. this). It is a stabilization-calibration choice: the conventions differ by O(1) factors (e.g. sqrt(2) between min-edge and diameter of a right-isoceles triangle) and rescale tau.

c1_multiplier.description: Multiplier on the equal-order constant c_1 = 4*k_v^4 returned by get_c1_c2 (paper Remark after eq:conditions_on_num_param); the effective constant is c1_multiplier * 4*k_v^4. c_1 is element-family dependent through the coercivity condition c_1 > 2*xi*C_inv^2: the paper takes c_1 = 4k^4 on the 2D triangular/quadrilateral meshes (c1_multiplier = 1.0, article.tex sec:NumericalExamplesStationaryCase2D) and c_1 = 16k^4 on the 3D tetrahedral meshes (c1_multiplier = 4.0, sec:NumericalExamplesStationaryCase3D), because C_inv is markedly larger for tets. NOTE c_1 enters tau_1/tau_2 in the DENOMINATOR, so a LARGER c_1 gives SMALLER tau, i.e. weaker stabilization.

c2_multiplier.description: Multiplier on the equal-order constant c_2 = 2*k_v^2 returned by get_c1_c2; the effective constant is c2_multiplier * 2*k_v^2. The paper keeps c_2 = 2k^2 in both 2D and 3D (c2_multiplier = 1.0); it is exposed alongside c1_multiplier so the (c_1, c_2) pair is recorded together in every result provenance record.

solver.title: Solver Configuration
solver.description: Hyperparameters controlling the Picard and Exact Newton linear/nonlinear solvers.

picard_iterations.description: Initial number of Picard iterations before switching to Newton.

xtol.description: Relative norm tolerance for the solution increment (dx).

picard_ftol.description: Residual tolerance for the Picard smoother to declare 'good enough' and hand off to Newton. Should be looser than ftol since Picard is a globalization aid, not a precise solver. Must be >= ftol.

eps_tol_momentum.description: Scale-free momentum tolerance tol_M: the nonlinear solve is converged when eps_M = ||r_M||/D_M <= tol_M, where D_M is the dynamic force-magnitude envelope (convergence_criterion.jl). Segment-independent (no per-segment re-anchoring). eps_M -> 0 at the discrete solution.

eps_tol_mass.description: Scale-free mass tolerance tol_C (Philosophy A, SYMMETRIC with eps_tol_momentum): converged when eps_C = ||r_C||/D_C <= tol_C, where r_C is the pressure-block of the assembled stabilized residual (the weak continuity residual the solver drives to zero) and D_C is the Galerkin mass-term envelope (convergence_criterion.jl). eps_C -> 0 at the discrete solution just like eps_M, so set tol_C as tight as eps_tol_momentum (the mass equation is now brought down the same way as momentum). The former strong-form measure ||eps*p + div(alpha u) - g||/(||grad(alpha u)|| + ||g||), which floors at O(h^kv), is kept as the DIAGNOSTIC eps_C_strong only (NOT gated); the pure-divergence ratio ||div(alpha u)||/||grad(alpha u)|| <= sqrt(d) remains a separate self-check.

max_increases.description: Number of successive residual increases before divergence abort.

freeze_jacobian_cusp.description: If true, freezes the magnitude of velocity in the nonlinearity Jacobian cross-terms.

linesearch_alpha_min.description: Minimum step size allowed before linesearch fails.

armijo_c1.description: Armijo sufficient decrease parameter (c1) for the linesearch.

divergence_merit_factor.description: Factor by which the merit function is allowed to increase before counting as a divergence step.

stagnation_noise_floor.description: The threshold for the residual inf-norm below which continuous divergence stops safely rather than crashing the solver.

condition_noise_floor_baseline.description: Baseline coefficient for the condition-aware noise floor (multiplies a system-conditioning estimate to produce the effective floor).

condition_noise_floor_absolute_min.description: Hard lower bound for the condition-aware noise floor (prevents it from collapsing to numerical zero).

condition_noise_floor_safety_factor.description: Safety factor (>= 1) applied to the condition-aware noise floor before use.

noise_floor_success_max_ftol_multiple.description: [honest-exit] A noise-floor solver stop is reported as CONVERGED only if the final residual inf-norm is <= this multiple of the (dynamic) ftol. Must be >= 1. Use a very large value (e.g. 1e30) to disable the gate (legacy behaviour: any noise-floor stop counts as success); use a finite value (e.g. 10.0) to reject high-Re fold stalls where the residual sits ~10^2-10^3x above ftol from masquerading as a true root.

max_linesearch_iterations.description: The strict upper limit mapped structurally dynamically onto exact armijo linesearch cycles avoiding topological float exhaustion.

linesearch_contraction_factor.description: Linesearch scalar parameter tracking geometric bounds across inner iterations recursively bounding unconstrained domains. (alpha *= factor).

run_diagnostics.description: Enable expanded diagnostics / debug print statements.

ablation_mode.description: Switch behavior of specific solver terms for systematic testing. 'picard_only' forces the Picard/Oseen Jacobian for every nonlinear stage (diagnostic: tests whether a converged result is solver-independent).

experimental_reaction_mode.description: Toggles advanced reaction handling behaviors for high-Re modes.

newton_stall_window.description: [iterator-scheduling P1] Consecutive non-improving Newton iterations before bailing to the cascade's Picard stage (stop_reason 'no_progress_stall'). 0 disables the guard (default; Newton runs its full budget = today's behaviour). Use >= 2 so a one-step plateau before quadratic descent is not bailed.

newton_stall_min_rel_improvement.description: [iterator-scheduling P1] Relative ||R||_inf reduction that counts as 'progress' for the stall window. In [0, 1). Keep small (e.g. 1e-3) so genuine quadratic descent always resets the window.

pingpong_enabled.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Master switch for the adaptive Newton<->Picard ping-pong cascade. false (default) keeps the one-way Newton->Picard->Newton cascade (today's behaviour).

pingpong_max_swaps.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Hard cap on Newton<->Picard alternations. 0 (with pingpong_enabled=false) is inert. Validate >= 0.

pingpong_picard_gain_orders.description: [iterator-scheduling P4] Orders of magnitude ||R||_inf must drop during a Picard segment before handing back to Newton (||R||_inf <= ||R0||_inf * 10^-gain). Read only when pingpong_enabled=true. Validate > 0.

newton_residual_divergence_patience.description: [divergence guard] Consecutive Newton iterations whose residual ||R||_inf rises beyond divergence_merit_factor before the Newton solve aborts and the cascade hands off to Picard. The :newton divergence safeguard otherwise watches the merit Phi, which the Armijo line search forces down, so a residual-divergent solve runs its full iteration budget. 0 (default) disables this guard (legacy). Validate >= 0.

osgs_anderson_enabled.description: Opt in to Anderson-accelerated OSGS (osgs_solver.jl). OFF by default: when disabled the OSGS coupled solve is the existing single inexact-Newton path (bit-identical). When enabled, the OSGS stage runs a staggered outer fixed-point (freeze pi, solve, re-project) with Anderson mixing of the (u,p) iterate.

osgs_anderson_depth.description: Anderson history depth m: how many past consecutive differences are mixed in the extrapolation. Used only when osgs_anderson_enabled.

osgs_anderson_relaxation.description: Anderson relaxation/mixing factor beta applied to the fixed-point residual. Used only when osgs_anderson_enabled.

osgs_anderson_safety_factor.description: Powell-style safeguard threshold: an extrapolation that moves farther than safety_factor times the residual norm is rejected and the Anderson history is reset. Used only when osgs_anderson_enabled.

osgs_anderson_max_outer.description: Cap on the staggered Anderson outer iterations. Used only when osgs_anderson_enabled.

osgs_jfnk_enabled.description: Opt in to Jacobian-Free Newton-Krylov for the OSGS coupled solve (osgs_solver.jl). OFF by default: when disabled the OSGS coupled solve is the existing single inexact-Newton path (bit-identical). Mutually exclusive with osgs_anderson_enabled. When enabled, the coupled Newton step solves the FULL tangent (recovering the dropped dpi/du coupling) via a matrix-free inner GMRES whose mat-vec is the directional finite difference of the re-projecting residual, preconditioned by the already-assembled+factored frozen-pi Jacobian. Phase-0 gate (docs/solver/jfnk-phase0-preconditioner-gate.md) measured this preconditioner viable.

osgs_jfnk_gmres_rel_tol.description: Inner-GMRES forcing tolerance eta (inexact Newton) for the matrix-free full-tangent solve. Must be in (0,1); ~1e-2 is the inexact-Newton inner tol. Used only when osgs_jfnk_enabled.

osgs_jfnk_gmres_maxiter.description: Cap on inner-GMRES iterations per JFNK Newton step. On non-convergence the step is rejected (C.1 honesty) and the solve falls back to the frozen-pi path. Used only when osgs_jfnk_enabled.

osgs_jfnk_gmres_restart.description: GMRES restart length (Krylov subspace size before restart) for the JFNK inner solve. Used only when osgs_jfnk_enabled.

osgs_jfnk_fd_epsilon.description: Brown-Saad finite-difference base b in the directional-derivative step eps = b*(1+norm(U))/norm(v) used for the matrix-free mat-vec. ~sqrt(machine eps) approx 1e-8. Must be > 0. Used only when osgs_jfnk_enabled.

osgs_jfnk_precond_c1_mult.description: Preconditioner-only c1 inflation for the JFNK inner solve: the frozen-pi preconditioner is assembled with c1*this (and c2*this) while the residual F and its matrix-free full tangent stay at the physical c1, so the converged SOLUTION is unchanged (paper-c1 root). A larger preconditioner c1 shrinks the dropped dpi/du coupling relative to the preconditioner (rho(J_pc^-1 * dpi/du) falls ~1178->3.8 at x4 in 3D-P2), letting inner GMRES converge where a c1=paper preconditioner cannot. 1.0 = off (byte-identical). Must be > 0. Used only when osgs_jfnk_enabled.

iterative_penalty_enabled.description: Opt in to the Codina iterative-penalty method (article.tex section 5.2, codina1993iterative). OFF by default (bit-identical: the mass residual carries no epsilon term, only the legacy Jacobian numerical_epsilon*dp). When enabled AND numerical_epsilon > 0, the mass-equation residual carries numerical_epsilon*(p^n - p^{n-1}) (LHS numerical_epsilon*p^n plus lagged RHS numerical_epsilon*p^{n-1}), solved as an OUTER fixed-point loop that holds p^{n-1} fixed within each pass and updates it between passes. It pins the constant-pressure null mode (well-posedness; the 3D all-Dirichlet case is ill-posed at epsilon=0) and vanishes at convergence (p^n=p^{n-1}), so the converged solution is unaltered. The matching numerical_epsilon*dp is already in the Jacobian.

iterative_penalty_max_iters.description: Cap on the outer iterative-penalty passes (each pass is a full nonlinear solve with p^{n-1} held fixed). The outer loop also stops early when the relative pressure drift falls below xtol. Used only when iterative_penalty_enabled.

recenter_pressure_between_penalty_passes.description: GAUGE-FREE HARDENING (theory/pressure_recentering_note). OFF by default (bit-identical: p^{n-1} stored verbatim). When ON, the outer iterative-penalty loop subtracts the domain mean int(p)/|Omega| from p^n before storing it as the lag p^{n-1}, bounding the pressure-mean drift -rho/(numerical_epsilon*|Omega|) that the all-Dirichlet gauge otherwise accumulates. Provenance: the iterated penalty is Codina's; the between-pass RE-CENTERING is a proposal of that note, NOT attributed to Codina and applied ONLY here. Changes no mean-removed quantity. Requires iterative_penalty_enabled=true and physical_epsilon==0 (gauge-free; with physical epsilon>0 the pressure level is anchored and re-centering is not exact) — both enforced in validate!.

osgs_skip_asgs_boot.description: Skip the ASGS Stage-I boot before the OSGS coupled/staggered solve. OFF by default (the boot runs, bit-identical). The boot is a code-side globalization safeguard, not part of the paper algorithm (which runs the OSGS staggered iteration directly from the initial guess). For a warm/exact guess it converges ASGS to the ASGS root first (a different fixed point) from which OSGS can be ill-conditioned. When an eps_pert homotopy provides cold-start globalization, skipping the boot (OSGS solves straight from the guess) is paper-faithful. No effect on ASGS-method runs.

linear_solver.title: Linear (inner) solver backend
linear_solver.description: Backend for the monolithic (u,p) Jacobian solve of each Newton/Picard iterate. The choice does not change the converged solution; it trades robustness/speed against peak memory. 'LU' = exact sparse direct solver (default, previously hardcoded). 'ILU_GMRES' = restarted GMRES left-preconditioned by an incomplete-LU factor, far lower peak memory for large 3D systems whose LU fill-in would exhaust RAM. The ilu_*/gmres_* fields are required even for LU (no silent default) but consumed only when method=ILU_GMRES.

method.description: Inner linear solver: 'LU' (direct, exact) or 'ILU_GMRES' (low-memory iterative).

ilu_drop_tolerance.description: ILU drop tolerance tau (smaller => denser/stronger preconditioner). Used only for ILU_GMRES.

gmres_restart.description: GMRES Krylov subspace size before restart. Used only for ILU_GMRES.

gmres_rel_tol.description: GMRES relative residual tolerance. Used only for ILU_GMRES.

gmres_maxiter.description: Cap on GMRES iterations. Used only for ILU_GMRES.

allow_unpreconditioned_fallback.description: [C.1] Policy when the ILU factorization fails (ILU_GMRES only). false (safe default): the linear solve fails loudly so the nonlinear cascade rolls back / falls to Picard. true: GMRES runs UNPRECONDITIONED (identity preconditioner) after a loud warning. Either way a GMRES that does not reach gmres_rel_tol within gmres_maxiter is reported as a FAILED step, never accepted as exact. Required even for LU (consumed only by ILU_GMRES).

partition.description: Number of elements in each direction [nx, ny].

convergence_partitions.description: Set of partitions for convergence studies (e.g., MMS tests).

output.title: Output Configuration
output.description: Path and naming for saving results.

basename.description: Prefix applied to saved solution files.

max_n_pert.description: Maximum noise permutations for MMS execution config.

epsilon_pert.description: Epsilon perturbations scale for condition tests.

h5_filename.description: Filename of H5 results database used in manufactured solutions.
